title: What a Real Math-Based TA Algo Actually Looks Like (Core Value Example)
author: Corevaluecapital
selftext:
Here’s an example of what a real math-based, TA-driven algorithm actually looks like in live trading. No filters, no only showing the winners. We didn’t win every trade, and that’s exactly how the system is supposed to work.



The Core Value System is entirely formula-driven. Every decision comes from math and structure:

– Momentum formulas

– Directional formulas

– Multi-timeframe moving average logic

– ATR-based take profit calculations

– Strict prohibiting indicators that block bad conditions

– Tier-based position sizing controlled by specific rules



Nothing is random, nothing is emotional. When the formulas give a valid score and all prohibiting checks are clear, the algo takes the trade. When the math says no, the trade is blocked, even if it looks good to the eye.



This batch of XAGUSD trades is a perfect example: a mix of wins and losses, managed risk, and a net positive outcome simply by following the rules consistently.



If anyone wants a deeper breakdown of how the formulas work or how the system processes structure in real time, I’m happy to share more.
